Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to find cases that contain a specific direct sequence of three activities Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in that exact order consecutively no other event from the same case in between

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For each case order by timestamp We need to find the sequence pattern Since we need consecutive events we can use LEAD or LAG window functions to check three consecutive rows Or we can use self joins with row numbering

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets define a subquery that assigns a row number to each event within each case ordered by timestamp Then we can look for rows where activity Approve Purchase Requisition and the next row has activity Create Order and the next has activity Confirm Order

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Approach Use a CTE with row number Then self join or use LEAD twice

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Alternative Use LEAD window function twice to get next activity and next timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The prompt says the time elapsed between the Create Order event and the Confirm Order event of that sequence is more than 5 days So if difference greater than 5 days We should consider that if there are multiple occurrences of the sequence in the same case we only need to exclude the case if any of those sequences meets the greater than 5 day condition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After identifying such cases exclude them from final result So final result returns all events from event log except those that belong to these cases

Name: Validation - IND
Text: There might be events with the exact same timestamp The row numbering will arbitrarily assign order but if timestamp ties the order is nondeterministic A better approach might be to use a secondary order by some unique key if exists but we do not know

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: This is cleaner and directly computes time between Create and Confirm The LEAD approach will handle the sequence check and timing condition directly
